LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PHILIP YUEN'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED, AND THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ARE VACATED AS VOID ON THEIR FACE PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 473(D). -SEE MINI MINUTES FOR DETAILS.- THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ARE VACATED. PLAINTIFF SHALL CAUSE TO BE RETURNED THE SUM OF $25,623.43 PREVIOUSLY LEVIED TO DEFENDANT WITHIN 10 DAYS OF THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. DEFENDANT SHALL PROVIDE AN ADDRESS AT WHICH HE CAN ACCEPT SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR AUTHORIZE HIS COUNSEL TO ACCEPT SERVICE ON HIS BEHALF. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Set Aside
Off Calendar Jun-18-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Greg Groeneveld, esq. (415-308-3637) for Plaintiff.
Joshua A. Ridless, esq. (415-614-2600) for Defendant.

The Court discloses that Mr. Groeneveld serves as a Judge Pro Tem in the Discovery department of this court, and his service does not effect the Court's ability to rule impartially.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript), Mr. Ridless states that he will authorize to accept service on behalf of his client. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT PHILIP YUEN's Motion To Set Aside entry of default judgment:

Defendant Philip Yuen's motion to set aside default judgment is granted, and the default and default judgment are vacated as void on their face pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, 473(d). Although substitute service on an individual may be proper at the defendant's "usual mailing address," before an individual may be served by substitute service, the burden is on the plaintiff to show that the summons and complaint "cannot with reasonable diligence be personally delivered to the person to be served." Code Civ. Proc. 415.20(b). Here, the proof of service is facially defective because it does not show that Plaintiff exercised reasonable diligence to serve plaintiff personally at a "proper place" or attempted to locate a proper place. (See American Express Centurion Bank v. Zara (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 383, 389 ["Two or three attempts to personally serve a defendant at a proper place ordinarily qualifies as "'reasonable diligence.'"]; cf. Hearn v. Howard (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 1193, 1202 [process server attempted to personally serve defendant at the business address on her letterhead and reported by the California State Bar by appearing at that address on three separate occasions]; Ellard v. Conway (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 540, 545-547 [process server attempted to serve defendants at their last known residence, discovered they had moved, and left copy of summons and complaint at their forwarding address, a private post office, and mailed the documents to the same place].)Although it appears that Defendant was aware of the litigation, actual notice is irrelevant on a facial challenge of the judgement and in any event is insufficient alone to confer jurisdiction in the absence of proper service. (See American Express Centurion Bank v. Zara, 199 Cal.App.4th at 392 ["Actual notice of the action alone, however, is not a substitute for proper service and is not sufficient to confer jurisdiction."].)

The default and default judgment are vacated. Plaintiff shall cause to be returned the sum of $25,623.43 previously levied to Defendant within 10 days of the date of this order. Defendant shall provide an address at which he can accept service of summons or authorize his counsel to accept service on his behalf.

Order is signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
